title: Mystery of the Ark of the Covenant
description:
The Ark of the Covenant is one of the most powerful and mysterious objects in the Bible. Built according to the measurements given by God during Moses' time, it was made of acacia wood and overlaid with pure gold inside and out. On top of it was the Mercy Seat with two cherubim spreading their wings. This Ark contained the stone tablets of the Ten Commandments, Aaron's rod that budded, and a golden pot of manna. It was the ultimate symbol of God's direct presence and His throne among the Israelites.

[IMG1]

This Ark possessed immense divine power. When the priests carrying it stepped into the Jordan River, the waters parted into two. When it was carried around the city of Jericho, the massive walls collapsed on their own. Once, when the Philistines captured it and placed it in the temple of their god Dagon, the idol fell on its face and broke into pieces before the Ark. As long as the Ark was with them, the Israelites achieved unstoppable victories in battles.

[IMG2]

However, herein lies a great mystery. The Ark was safely kept in the magnificent temple built by King Solomon. But in 587 BC, when King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon destroyed Jerusalem and looted the temple's treasures, the Ark of the Covenant was nowhere on the list of stolen items. There is no mention in the Bible of it being captured or destroyed by enemies. From that time on, what happened to the Ark and where it was hidden remains one of the greatest unsolved mysteries. Yet, a fascinating truth is revealed in Revelation 11:19, where the Apostle John had a vision of the Ark of His Covenant in God's temple in heaven.

[IMG3]

title: The Mystery of Melchizedek - The Eternal Priest
description:
In the book of Genesis, a highly mysterious figure named 'Melchizedek' suddenly appears and then vanishes. When Abraham was returning victorious after rescuing his nephew Lot and defeating the kings, Melchizedek, the King of Salem (Jerusalem), came out to meet him. He was the priest of God Most High. He brought out bread and wine and blessed Abraham. In return, Abraham gave him a tithe (a tenth) of all the spoils he had gathered.

[IMG1]

The name Melchizedek translates to 'King of Righteousness', and King of Salem means 'King of Peace'. A profound mystery about him is revealed in the New Testament in Hebrews chapter 7. The Bible clearly states that Melchizedek was without father or mother, without genealogy, without beginning of days or end of life. Resembling the Son of God, he remains a priest forever.

[IMG2]

For a human being to have no parents, birth, or death is a mystery beyond human comprehension. This is why many biblical scholars strongly believe that Melchizedek was a pre-incarnate appearance of Jesus Christ in the Old Testament. The bread and wine he brought vividly symbolize the Lord's Supper (communion). This incredible mystery is further strengthened in Psalm 110:4, where God makes an oath concerning the coming Messiah: "You are a priest forever, in the order of Melchizedek."

[IMG3]
